title: Creations
description: Here are a few of the things I've designed over the years

lego.title: Hindustani Temple - Kandariya Mahadev
lego.subtitle: Khajuraho, India
lego.description: Kandariya Mahadeva, meaning 'Great God of the Cave', is a Hindu temple at Khajuraho in Madhya Pradesh, India. Famous for its ornate statutary, it is considered one of the best intact examples of royal temples from the medieval period. The temple was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1986. 4237 total pieces.

lego.title: Turkish Mosque - Aya Sofya
lego.subtitle: Istanbul, Turkiye
lego.description: Aya Sofya, originally known by the Greek name 'Hagia Sophia' or 'Holy Wisdom', was built as a cathedral under the Eastern Roman Emperor Justinian I in the 6th Century A.D. At the time of its completion, it was the world's largest interior space topped by the world's largest dome. After Istanbul's conquest by the Turkish Sultan Mehmed II in 1453, it was converted into a mosque. It was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1985. 2260 total pieces.

lego.title: Orthodox Church - Kizhi Pogost
lego.subtitle: Lake Ladoga, Russia
lego.description: Kizhi Pogost is a historical wooden church located on Lake Onega in northern Russia dating from the 17th century. With twenty-two domes, the Transfiguration Church is the largest wooden structure in the world build without structural nails. It was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1990. 3166 total pieces.

lego.title: Mughal Tomb - Taj Mahal
lego.subtitle: Agra, India
lego.description: Taj Mahal is a white marble mausoleum commissioned in 1631 A.D. by the fifth Mughal emperor Shah Jahan to commemorate his deceased wife, Mumtaz Mahal. Widely considered the crowning jewel of Indo-Islamic architecture, the complex of white marble and red sandstone buildings is located outside Agra on the Yamuna River, and includes a mosque and guest house set within a walled Persian-style garden. It designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983. 2997 total pieces.

lego.title: Dravidian Temple - Meenakshi Gopuram
lego.subtitle: Madurai, India
lego.description: Meenakshi Temple is an ancient Hindu religious complex named after a goddess who forms the spiritual nucleus of the pilgrimage city of Madurai in Tamil Nadu, India. It is widely recognized for its fourteen intricately-decorated gateway towers, or Gopurams, which reach as high as 52 meters and crown a series of nested courtyards leading to the main idol of the deity. The current restored structure dates from the 16th Century A.D., and continues to serve as a popular symbol of Tamil identity. 5444 total pieces.

lego.title: Persian Tomb - Gur-i Amir
lego.subtitle: Samarkand, Uzbekistan
lego.description: Gur-i Amir is a medieval Islamic tomb located in Samarkand, Uzbekistan. The tomb was originally built to house the remains of Tamerlane's grandson Muhammad Sultan, but was appropriated as the conqueror's own mausoleum following his death in 1405 A.D. The monument is crowned by a striking turquoise dome, and its structure serves as the prototype of the Timurid architectural style which greatly influenced later Persian and Indian monumental design. 4772 pieces.

aoe2.title: Silk Roads - Crossroads of Civilization
aoe2.subtitle: Medieval Central Asia
aoe2.description: A playable recreation of Central Asia from the 8th-17th Centuries A.D. including major cities and landmarks such as Samarkand, Delhi, Nishapur, and Lanzhou. Simulate the perennial rise and fall of medieval nomadic empires, including the Gokturks, Uighurs, Seljuks, Qarakhitans, and Qarakhanids. Players can relive the adventures of Tughril Beg, Mahmud of Ghazni, Ghiyathuddin Tughluq, Genghis Khan, Tamerlane,  and even the Tang Dynasty's "Pacification of the West" under Su Dingfang.

aoe2.title: Mediterranean - The Ancient World
aoe2.subtitle: Near East in Classical Antiquity
aoe2.description: A playable recreation of the Fertile Crescent, Asia Minor, and Eastern Mediterranean from the 20th-1st Centuries B.C. Simulate the rise and fall of the Egyptian New Kingdom, Greek and Phoenician city-states, and successive Babylonian and Assyrian empires. Govern the world's first complex societies from Memphis and Thebes, Athens and Sparta, Babylon, Nineveh, and Carthage.

aoe2.title: Chinese Dynasties - The Mandate of Heaven
aoe2.subtitle: Late Classical China
aoe2.description: A playable recreation of the "Middle Kingdom" from the 3rd-13th Centuries A.D. centered on the traditional capitals of Chang'an, Chengdu, Luoyang, and Hangzhou. Simulate the consolidation of power following the Three Kingdoms period, or the barbarian invasions of the Tang, Sui, and Sung empires. While fending off barbarian invasions from the northern steppes, contend with the powerful Kingdom of Dali in the south and incessant raids by Japanese pirates along the coasts.

aoe2.title: Southern Seas - The Spice Trade
aoe2.subtitle: South Asia in the Age of Discovery
aoe2.description: A playable recreation of coastal India, Arabia, and Southeast Asia from the 9th-16th Centuries A.D. Simulate the conquests of Rajendra Chola, the voyages of Zheng He's treasure fleet, and the discoveries of the Portuguese explorers. Secure control of resource-rich coastal areas by fortifying key ports including Hormuz, Khambat/Surat, Goa, Cochin, Nagapattinam, Chittagong, Pegu, Kedah, Saigon, and Guangzhou.
